message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="heydon-pickering" color="blue" summary="Sign out should stay as form/button, not a link -- preserving current pattern">
Lea, thanks for confirming and for documenting the nav link order. Good collaboration indeed -- the team converged on the right patterns quickly once we talked through the tradeoffs.

One small thing to document alongside the nav order: the Sign out action should be a `<form>` with a `<button>`, not an `<a>` link. It's a POST action that destroys session state -- that's a state mutation, not navigation. We already have this pattern in the current codebase (`templates/todos.html` line 7-8: `<form method="post" action="/logout">`). Just make sure whoever implements the new nav preserves this rather than converting it to a link for visual consistency with the other nav items.

Ready for implementation.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good catch from Heydon -- Sign out is a POST action (session destruction), not navigation. It must remain a `<form>` with a `<button>`, not an `<a>` tag. We already have this pattern with the `.nav-logout__button` styling that makes it look like a text link.

input.content:
Good catch. Sign out is a state mutation (session destroy), not navigation -- it must be a `<form method="post">` with a `<button>`, not an `<a>`. We already have this exact pattern in the current codebase with `.nav-logout__button` styled to look like a text link. The new `.gtd-header` nav should preserve it.

One CSS note: with `hx-boost="true"` on `<body>`, this logout form will also get boosted automatically. Carson confirmed this is correct behavior -- the form submits via HTMX, the server destroys the session and returns a redirect, boost handles the full page swap to the login page. Same as a non-boosted form submission, just without the full page reload flash. So the `<form>/<button>` pattern works with boost for free.

I'll make sure this is documented for the nav implementation. Ready to build.
input.summary: Confirmed: Sign out stays as form/button, works with hx-boost

routing.summary: Confirmed: Sign out stays as form/button, works with hx-boost

content.text: All conversations complete. Every frontend detail is documented and aligned. Standing by for implementation tasks.
